Melancthon the life of Philip Melancthon , who die Ano Christi 1560. fillip Melancthon be bear at Bretta in the low palatinate , anno christi 1497. his birth and education . of honest parent , of a middle rank , by who he be set to school to learn grammar of John Hungarus , a learned and honest man ; and to learn Greek of George Simler ; and afterward they send he to Heidleberg at twelve year of age , where he have logic and physics read to he , he go to Heidleberge . and attain ability to make a verse , he fall to read poetry and history . there also , be very young , be instruct the son of count Leonstein , and commence bachelor of art anno christi 1511. and of his age fourteen , with general approbation . after three year study there , the air not agree with he , and be deny his degree of master of art , by reason of his youth , remove to tube , he go to tube . anno 1512. he always use to carry his bible about with he , read often in it both at church and elsewhere , yet be he carry away with the common error of the time , of which himself say , i •remble to think with what blind devotion i go to image , while i be a papist . he pivate at tube teach the law , and read over very diligent all the book of Gallen , and with Oecolampadius read over Hesiod . his employment . he stay in that university four year , where he profit so much in the art , tongue , and philosophy , that he read public lecture in the school out of Virgil , Terence , and Tully , and be employ in oversee the press . he study the mathematics , law , and physic , in all which he profit much . there he commence master of art , mr. of art anno christi 1513. and of his age , seventeen . Cato major : or , the book of old age . first write by M.t. Cicero . and now excellent english by William Austin of lincolns inn , esquire . with annotation upon the name of the man and place . London , print for William Leake , and be to be sell at his shop at the sign of the crown in Fleetstreet , between the two temple gate . 1648. the stationer to the reader . this most excellent book , entitle Cato Major de senectute , little in bulk , but fill with pithy , and substantial diseourse , smooth couch , elegant and delightful convey in every line , write long since in the latin tongue , by that most rare and grave consul and senator of Rome , the glory of his time , m. T. Cicero , prince of orator ; needs no man commendation , ( all his work be so famous and renown among the learned , ) so that i may well be silent , have not i judge it fit to give the reader a account how it come in this manner to be divulge ; it fall accidental into my hand , and i find the translation and note to be the labour of a worthy gentleman of great part , learning and sufficiency , who name be thereunto : and the book it self , for variety of elegant passage and deep philosophical discourse , be both delight full and useful ; personate by the most learned , ancient , and grave senator , grandee of that age , abase and depress all youthful lust , commend and strengthen the venerable life of old age , show the current of virtue wherein it ought to run , with the distance betwixt it and youth , and how many way it do exceed the same , with the advantage it have beyond youth ; declare how they slight death in this their age , comfort themselves in all its imperfection , with that expect immortality at hand , the more age they then be . and have receive the approbation of this learned piece , from diverse person of judgement and understanding , i be upon these and other like consideration , move for the general good , to publish it thus abroad unto the world , believe that it will be a delight to the age , and a great benefit to the unlearned in the latin tongue , who may in their own language read the sage wisdom of former time , and see how the infirmity of age be recompense with more rare perfection of mind , and that it do not so much destroy , as change the delight of youth for better ; and so be prepare to bid that welcome which be approach towards they . if the excellency of this work , with the worthiness of the author and translator , shall gain acceptation with the lover of wisdom , it will answer the expectation , and reward thc care of the publisher .
